It would expand voting access, end partisan gerrymandering, and shine a light on dark money in elections. The communities I represent — working-class families, immigrants, people of color — have the most to gain when every vote counts equally.
source_name: U.S. Congress — For the People Act (H.R. 1)

quote_text: Housing is a human right, and the lack of affordable housing is one of the greatest crises facing working families in Los Angeles and across the country. I have introduced legislation to create a federal housing guarantee because we cannot solve homelessness and housing insecurity with incremental measures.

I am the only member of Congress with a computer science degree, and I can tell you that artificial intelligence presents extraordinary opportunities and extraordinary risks. We need a federal AI regulatory framework that promotes innovation while preventing harms — particularly around autonomous weapons, deepfakes, and algorithmic discrimination. Congress cannot wait for the next crisis to act.

Medicare for All is not a radical idea — it is what the majority of Americans want. Every other wealthy country has figured out how to provide universal health care. We can do it too, and H.R. 1384 shows exactly how. The question is not whether we can afford it; the question is whether we can afford not to.
source_name: U.S. Congress — Medicare for All Act (H.R. 1384)

quote_text: As a legal immigrant who came to this country from Taiwan as a child, I know that immigration is the story of America. The American Dream and Promise Act would finally deliver the certainty that Dreamers deserve. These are Americans in every way except on paper, and it is long past time we make it official.
source_name: U.S. Congress — American Dream and Promise Act (H.R. 6)

quote_text: Authoritarian aggression cannot go unanswered. I voted for the Ukraine supplemental because American leadership in the face of Putin's war is essential — not just for Ukraine but for Taiwan and every democracy that looks to the United States as a partner and guarantor of international order.

The George Floyd Justice in Policing Act is about accountability, transparency, and the principle that no one in America is above the law — not even law enforcement. As a former military JAG officer, I understand that accountability is the foundation of trust, and trust is the foundation of legitimate government.
source_name: U.S. Congress — George Floyd Justice in Policing Act (H.R. 7120)

quote_text: Abortion is health care. Congress must restore the constitutional protection for reproductive freedom that the Supreme Court eliminated in Dobbs. I am a proud cosponsor of the Women's Health Protection Act because the decision of whether and when to have a child belongs to the woman — not to politicians in Washington or in any state capital.
source_name: U.S. Congress — Women's Health Protection Act (H.R. 3755)

quote_text: Democracy requires that every eligible voter can actually vote. The For the People Act would modernize our elections, protect against voter suppression, end partisan gerrymandering, and require disclosure of dark money. I am proud to be a cosponsor because a democracy that makes it harder to vote is a democracy in name only.
source_name: U.S. Congress — For the People Act (H.R. 1)

quote_text: Criminal justice reform is a racial justice imperative. The communities I represent in South Los Angeles have borne the weight of over-policing and under-investment for generations. I came to Congress to change that — to pass meaningful accountability legislation and redirect resources toward housing, mental health, and education.
source_name: U.S. Congress — George Floyd Justice in Policing Act (H.R. 7120)

quote_text: Health care is a right, not a privilege, and the Medicare for All Act is the framework that finally treats it that way. Every person in my district deserves access to affordable, comprehensive care regardless of their zip code or immigration status. I was proud to become a cosponsor the moment I arrived in Congress.
source_name: U.S. Congress — Medicare for All Act (H.R. 1384)

quote_text: The Green New Deal is not just environmental policy — it is economic justice. The communities in CA-37, particularly in South LA, have been living next to oil refineries and industrial polluters for decades while bearing the health consequences. We need a just transition away from fossil fuels that creates clean energy jobs right here in the communities most harmed by pollution.

The Women's Health Protection Act is about restoring a fundamental constitutional right that the Supreme Court took away. As a Black woman representing a district where Black maternal mortality rates are already tragically high, I understand the stakes. Abortion access is not a political debate — it is a matter of life and death for women in my community.
source_name: U.S. Congress — Women's Health Protection Act (H.R. 3755)

quote_text: Our immigration system is broken because Congress has failed to act for decades. As a member of the Progressive Caucus, I am committed to passing comprehensive immigration reform that creates a path to citizenship, protects Dreamers, and ends the cruel and costly practice of mass deportation. The American Dream and Promise Act is a critical first step.
source_name: U.S. Congress — American Dream and Promise Act (H.R. 6)

quote_text: The right to vote is the cornerstone of our democracy, and we must protect it from the systematic attacks we have seen in state after state. The For the People Act would restore and strengthen voting rights, end partisan gerrymandering, and get money out of politics. Every vote should count equally — that is the promise of a democracy.
source_name: U.S. Congress — For the People Act (H.R. 1)

quote_text: Mass deportation is a humanitarian catastrophe and a moral failure. What the Trump administration is doing to immigrant communities — terrorizing families, separating children from parents — is deeply un-American. I will use every tool I have in Congress to stop it and to protect the people in my district who are living in fear.

As the first Latina to serve on the Ways and Means Committee, I have a seat at the table where health care policy gets made — and I intend to use it. H.R. 1384 would finally deliver on the promise that health care is a right. The workers and families in my district in the San Gabriel Valley deserve nothing less.
source_name: U.S. Congress — Medicare for All Act (H.R. 1384)

quote_text: I have been fighting for immigration reform my entire career in Congress. The American Dream and Promise Act is the right thing to do for the 800,000 Dreamers who have known no other home. As a daughter of Mexican immigrant parents, I take this personally — this is about my family's story and the story of millions of Americans.
source_name: U.S. Congress — American Dream and Promise Act (H.R. 6)

quote_text: Standing with Ukraine means standing for the principle that democracies cannot be allowed to fall to authoritarian aggression. I voted yes on the Ukraine supplemental because the cost of inaction — a Putin victory, an emboldened autocracy, a shattered alliance structure — is far greater than the cost of support.

The Women's Health Protection Act is about restoring the constitutional right to abortion care that the Supreme Court eliminated. Reproductive freedom is not a partisan issue — it is a fundamental matter of health, autonomy, and dignity. I am proud to cosponsor this legislation and I will keep fighting until it becomes law.
source_name: U.S. Congress — Women's Health Protection Act (H.R. 3755)

quote_text: Systemic racism is real, and the George Floyd Justice in Policing Act is a concrete step toward dismantling it in our criminal justice system. The diverse communities I represent — Latino, Asian American, Black working-class families — deserve a police force that serves and protects everyone equally. Accountability is not optional.
source_name: U.S. Congress — George Floyd Justice in Policing Act (H.R. 7120)

quote_text: The Respect for Marriage Act is about protecting families. LGBTQ+ families in my district and across the country deserve the security of knowing their marriages are protected under federal law regardless of what any future court may decide. Love is love, and the law should reflect that.
source_name: U.S. Congress — Respect for Marriage Act (S. 4556)

quote_text: The For the People Act is the most important voting rights bill in a generation. It expands access to the ballot, protects against suppression, ends partisan gerrymandering, and requires disclosure of dark money. The working families I represent cannot afford to have their votes drowned out by billionaires and special interests.
source_name: U.S. Congress — For the People Act (H.R. 1)

quote_text: I am not about to be intimidated or afraid. I have been fighting for civil rights my entire career, and I am not stopping now. The George Floyd Justice in Policing Act is a step in the right direction, but we need to go further — we need to defund the police and reinvest in the communities that have been neglected for generations.
source_name: U.S. Congress — George Floyd Justice in Policing Act (H.R. 7120)

quote_text: What the Trump administration is doing with these mass deportations is absolutely unconscionable. These are human beings — mothers, fathers, children — who have built lives in this country, who work hard every day, who pay taxes and contribute to our communities. I will not stand by while they are ripped from their homes and sent away.

Health care is a civil rights issue. Black and brown communities in my district and across America have been denied adequate health care for too long. I am a proud cosponsor of Medicare for All because every single person in this country — rich or poor, documented or undocumented — deserves access to quality health care.
source_name: U.S. Congress — Medicare for All Act (H.R. 1384)

quote_text: The housing crisis in Los Angeles is a humanitarian emergency. In Inglewood and Watts and the communities I have represented for over thirty years, I see families being pushed out by skyrocketing rents and developers who only care about profit. We need federal investment in affordable housing at a scale we have never seen before.

I voted yes on the Ukraine supplemental because we cannot allow Putin to succeed. The world is watching. If we abandon Ukraine, we signal to every authoritarian on earth that aggression pays. America must lead the free world, and that means keeping our commitment to Ukraine's sovereignty and independence.

The American Dream and Promise Act gives Dreamers the legal protection they deserve. These young people grew up in this country, went to our schools, serve in our military. They are American in every meaningful sense. I have been fighting for immigration reform for decades and I will not stop until they have a path to citizenship.
source_name: U.S. Congress — American Dream and Promise Act (H.R. 6)

quote_text: Voter suppression is voter intimidation, plain and simple. The For the People Act would tear down the walls that have been built to keep Black voters and communities of color away from the polls. We fought too hard and bled too much for the right to vote to allow it to be taken away without a fight.
source_name: U.S. Congress — For the People Act (H.R. 1)

quote_text: My district includes the Port of Los Angeles and communities that have lived with oil refineries and diesel pollution for generations. The Green New Deal is not an abstract concept for the people of CA-44 — it is a life-or-death matter. We need to transition off fossil fuels and create clean energy jobs in the communities that have suffered the most.

I am the daughter of immigrants, and I represent a district where immigration is not a political issue — it is the story of nearly every family. The American Dream and Promise Act is about protecting Dreamers and TPS holders who have contributed so much to our communities. Passing comprehensive immigration reform is one of my highest priorities.
source_name: U.S. Congress — American Dream and Promise Act (H.R. 6)

quote_text: The Medicare for All Act is the only comprehensive solution to our broken health care system. In the communities I represent — San Pedro, Compton, Carson, Lynwood — families are one medical bill away from financial ruin. Health care is a right, and H.R. 1384 is how we make that right a reality.
source_name: U.S. Congress — Medicare for All Act (H.R. 1384)

quote_text: Mass deportation is tearing families apart in my district. I have met with mothers whose children have been taken, with workers who are afraid to leave their homes. This is not the America I believe in. As a member of the Congressional Hispanic Caucus, I am committed to fighting these cruel policies every single day.

The George Floyd Justice in Policing Act would bring real accountability to law enforcement and protect communities that have been victims of police brutality for far too long. In Compton and Lynwood and the other communities in CA-44, residents deserve to be protected, not victimized, by the people sworn to serve them.
source_name: U.S. Congress — George Floyd Justice in Policing Act (H.R. 7120)

quote_text: Reproductive freedom is non-negotiable. Women in my district and across the country deserve the right to make their own health care decisions without government interference. As a cosponsor of the Women's Health Protection Act, I will keep fighting until we restore the protections that the Supreme Court eliminated in Dobbs.
source_name: U.S. Congress — Women's Health Protection Act (H.R. 3755)

quote_text: The For the People Act is the most consequential democracy reform bill in decades. It would expand early voting, make registration automatic, end partisan gerrymandering, and expose dark money in elections. The working-class communities I represent — communities of color, immigrant families, first-generation voters — need these protections.
source_name: U.S. Congress — For the People Act (H.R. 1)
